L. 100–203, (c), , , provided that, notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary of Health and Human Services was not authorized to issue, after , and before , any final regulation, instruction, or other policy change which was primarily intended to have the effect of slowing down claims processing, or delaying payment of claims, under this subchapter, and that , amending this section and and enacting provisions set out as notes under this section, was a necessary (but secondary) result of a significant policy change.Pub. Replacement of Agency, Organization, or Carrier Processing Medicare Claims; Number of Agreements and Contracts Authorized for Fiscal Years 1985 Through 1993, , , as amended by , , ; , , ; , , ; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–369, div. B, title III, § 2326(a)98 Stat. 1087Pub. L. 98–617, § 3(a)(2)98 Stat. 3295Pub. L. 99–509, title IX, § 9321(b)100 Stat. 2016Pub. L. 101–239, title VI, § 6215(a)103 Stat. 2252Pub. L. 103–432, title I, § 159(a)108 Stat. 4443July 18, 1984Nov. 8, 1984Oct. 21, 1986Dec. 19, 1989Oct. 31, 1994“During each fiscal year (beginning with fiscal year 1985 and ending with fiscal year 1993), the Secretary of Health and Human Services may enter into not more than two agreements under section 1816 of the Social Security Act [], and not more than two contracts under section 1842 of such Act [], on the basis of competitive bidding, without regard to the nominating process under section 1816(a) of such Act or cost reimbursement provisions under sections 1816(c) or 1842(c) of such Act during the term of the agreement. Such procedure may be used only for the purpose of replacing an agency or organization or carrier which over a 2-year period of time has been in the lowest 20th percentile of agencies and organizations or carriers having agreements or contracts under the respective section, as measured by the Secretary’s cost and performance criteria. In addition, beginning with fiscal year 1990 and any subsequent fiscal year the Secretary may enter into such additional agreements and contracts without regard to such cost reimbursement provisions if the fiscal intermediary or carrier involved and the Secretary agree to waive such provisions, but the Secretary may not take any action that has the effect of requiring that the intermediary or carrier agree to waive such provisions, including requiring such a waiver as a condition for entering into or renewing such an agreement or contract. Any agency or organization or carrier selected on the basis of competitive bidding must perform all of the duties listed in section 1816(a) of such Act, or the duties listed in paragraphs (1) through (4) of section 1842(a) of such Act, as the case may be, and must be a health insuring organization (as determined by the Secretary).”42 U.S.C. 1395h42 U.S.C. 1395u[, , , provided that: ]Pub. L. 103–432, title I, § 159(b)108 Stat. 4443Oct. 31, 1994“The amendment made by subsection (a) [amending , set out above] shall apply beginning with fiscal year 1994.”section 2326(a) of Pub. L. 98–369[, , , provided that: ]Pub. Audit and Medical Claims Review, , , as amended by , , , provided that, in addition to any funds otherwise provided for payments to intermediaries and carriers under agreements entered into under this section and , there were transferred from the Federal Hospital Insurance Trust Fund and the Federal Supplementary Medical Insurance Fund an additional $45,000,000 for each of fiscal years 1983, 1984, and 1985, and $105,000,000 for each of fiscal years 1986, 1987, and 1988 for payments to such intermediaries and carriers under such agreements to be used exclusively for purposes of carrying out provider cost audits, of reviewing medical necessity, and of recovering third-party liability payments.Pub. Replacement of Agency, Organization, or Carrier Processing Medicare Claims; Number of Agreements and Contracts Authorized for Fiscal Years 1985 Through 1993, , , as amended by , , ; , , ; , , ; , , , provided that:Pub. L. 98–369, div. B, title III, § 2326(a)98 Stat. 1087Pub. L. 98–617, § 3(a)(2)98 Stat. 3295Pub. L. 99–509, title IX, § 9321(b)100 Stat. 2016Pub. L. 101–239, title VI, § 6215(a)103 Stat. 2252Pub. L. 103–432, title I, § 159(a)108 Stat. 4443July 18, 1984Nov. 8, 1984Oct. 21, 1986Dec. 19, 1989Oct. 31, 1994“During each fiscal year (beginning with fiscal year 1985 and ending with fiscal year 1993), the Secretary of Health and Human Services may enter into not more than two agreements under section 1816 of the Social Security Act [], and not more than two contracts under section 1842 of such Act [], on the basis of competitive bidding, without regard to the nominating process under section 1816(a) of such Act or cost reimbursement provisions under sections 1816(c) or 1842(c) of such Act during the term of the agreement. Such procedure may be used only for the purpose of replacing an agency or organization or carrier which over a 2-year period of time has been in the lowest 20th percentile of agencies and organizations or carriers having agreements or contracts under the respective section, as measured by the Secretary’s cost and performance criteria. In addition, beginning with fiscal year 1990 and any subsequent fiscal year the Secretary may enter into such additional agreements and contracts without regard to such cost reimbursement provisions if the fiscal intermediary or carrier involved and the Secretary agree to waive such provisions, but the Secretary may not take any action that has the effect of requiring that the intermediary or carrier agree to waive such provisions, including requiring such a waiver as a condition for entering into or renewing such an agreement or contract. Any agency or organization or carrier selected on the basis of competitive bidding must perform all of the duties listed in section 1816(a) of such Act, or the duties listed in paragraphs (1) through (4) of section 1842(a) of such Act, as the case may be, and must be a health insuring organization (as determined by the Secretary).”42 U.S.C. 1395h42 U.S.C. 1395u[, , , provided that: ]Pub. children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The administration of this part shall be conducted through contracts with medicare administrative contractors under .section 1395kk–1 of this title

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 108–173, title IX, § 911(b)(3)117 Stat. 2383Dec. 8, 2003
children.label: b Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 108–173, title IX, § 911(b)(3)117 Stat. 2383Dec. 8, 2003
ii
children.text: for which payment is not made on a periodic interim payment basis,

children.label: i
children.text: The term “clean claim” means a claim that has no defect or impropriety (including any lack of any required substantiating documentation) or particular circumstance requiring special treatment that prevents timely payment from being made on the claim under this subchapter.

children.label: I
children.text: with respect to claims received in the 12-month period beginning , 30 calendar days,October 1, 1986

children.label: II
children.text: with respect to claims received in the 12-month period beginning , 26 calendar days,October 1, 1987

children.label: III
children.text: with respect to claims received in the 12-month period beginning , 25 calendar days,October 1, 1988

children.label: IV
children.text: with respect to claims received in the 12-month period beginning , and claims received in any succeeding 12-month period ending on or before , 24 calendar days, andOctober 1, 1989September 30, 1993

children.label: V
children.text: with respect to claims received in the 12-month period beginning , and claims received in any succeeding 12-month period, 30 calendar days.October 1, 1993

children.label: C
children.text: If payment is not issued, mailed, or otherwise transmitted within the applicable number of calendar days (as defined in clause (ii) of subparagraph (B)) after a clean claim (as defined in clause (i) of such subparagraph) is received from a hospital, critical access hospital, skilled nursing facility, home health agency, hospice program, comprehensive outpatient rehabilitation facility, or rehabilitation agency that is not receiving payments on a periodic interim payment basis with respect to such services, interest shall be paid at the rate used for purposes of (relating to interest penalties for failure to make prompt payments) for the period beginning on the day after the required payment date and ending on the date on which payment is made.section 3902(a) of title 31

children.label: A
children.text: Each contract under that provides for making payments under this part shall provide that no payment shall be issued, mailed, or otherwise transmitted with respect to any claim submitted under this subchapter within the applicable number of calendar days after the date on which the claim is received.section 1395kk–1 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: with respect to claims submitted electronically as prescribed by the Secretary, 13 days, and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to claims submitted otherwise, 28 days.

children.heading: to (i). Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 108–173, title IX, § 911(b)(5)117 Stat. 2383Dec. 8, 2003
children.label: d to (i). Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 108–173, title IX, § 911(b)(5)117 Stat. 2383Dec. 8, 2003

children.heading: Denial of claim; notification and reconsideration
children.label: j Denial of claim; notification and reconsideration

children.text: furnish the provider and the individual with respect to whom the claim is made with a written explanation of the denial and of the statutory or regulatory basis for the denial; and
in the case of a request for reconsideration of a denial, promptly notify such individual and the provider of the disposition of such reconsideration.

children.heading: Annual reporting requirement on erroneous payment recovery
children.label: k Annual reporting requirement on erroneous payment recovery
children.text: A contract with a medicare administrative contractor under with respect to the administration of this part shall require that such medicare administrative contractor submit an annual report to the Secretary describing the steps taken to recover payments made for items or services for which payment has been or could be made under a primary plan (as defined in ).section 1395kk–1 of this titlesection 1395y(b)(2)(A) of this title

children.heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 108–173, title IX, § 911(b)(7)117 Stat. 2383Dec. 8, 2003
children.label: l Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 108–173, title IX, § 911(b)(7)117 Stat. 2383Dec. 8, 2003
